headlines.title: More evidence shows shingles vaccine protects against dementia. What’s really going on?
headlines.description: Shingles, a condition triggered by the reactivation of the varicella-zoster virus in the body, can cause a “war zone” of inflammation in the brain.

headlines.title: Senators call for military healthcare to cover autism therapy as a basic benefit
headlines.description: Sens. Kirsten Gillibrand and Eric Schmitt issued a letter Friday to Pentagon leadership calling for the military healthcare program to cover autism therapy as a basic benefit.

headlines.title: This autism therapy is covered by many insurers. Why does the military restrict it?
headlines.description: TRICARE, the military’s healthcare program, limits coverage of applied behavior analysis, a type of therapy for people with autism. Kristi Cabiao is trying to get the policy changed.

headlines.title: As cancer death rates plunge, clear disparities remain, report says
headlines.description: Black and American Indian/Alaska Native communities have the highest cancer rates of any racial or ethnic group in the U.S.

headlines.title: Struggling to Focus? Here's How to Train Your Attention Span
headlines.description: In a world of constant distractions, it can be difficult to stay focused, so psychotherapist Niro Feliciano joins TODAY with tips on how to train your attention span. She advises setting time blocks, creating phone-free zones and touts the benefits of making lists.

headlines.title: How to Keep Your Skin Healthy and Hydrated in Summer Months
headlines.description: From sunburns to heat rashes, board-certified Dr. Angela Lamb joins TODAY with solutions for common summer skin problems.

headlines.title: Some in MAHA movement express frustration over Supreme Court ruling on Roundup weed killer
headlines.description: One member of the “Make America Healthy Again” movement said the decision was "sickening" after the Supreme Court ruled that Bayer, the manufacturer of Roundup, did not need to warn consumers of a potential cancer risk associated with its weed killer. NBC News’ Julie Tsirkin reports on the MAHA movement's frustration with the Supreme Court's ruling.

headlines.title: Family fights for coverage of autism therapy
headlines.description: In our series “The Cost of Denial”, Erin McLaughlin reports on the military family fighting to get their son coverage for the autism therapy that could help him live a more independent life.

headlines.title: Fitness trainers swear by weighted vests — here are the best ones
headlines.description: Do weighted vests really work? Here's why trainers swear by working out with ones from Omorpho, TRX, Go Ruck and more.

headlines.title: The best walking shoes for women on Amazon
headlines.description: We compiled a list of walking sneakers recommended by experts and NBC Select editors from brands like Nike, Asics and Saucony — all available on Amazon.

headlines.title: Can “super shoes” make you super speedy? Here’s what to know about carbon plate sneakers
headlines.description: Experts explain the benefits of carbon plate running shoes and who should wear them. Plus, learn the difference between super shoes for racing and training.

headlines.title: Is this $178 bra actually life-changing?
headlines.description: I tested the Forme Power Bra for five months to see if it could improve my posture and back support. Here’s what actually happened.
